name: Simple Stepper with Navigation Buttons
summary: Categorization with `variant: stepper` for a simple form stepper with customizable navigation buttons and step tabs
whenToUse: Moderate-complexity forms where a simple step navigation bar is sufficient
Internal staff forms where the full Task List pattern is not needed
When users are experienced and benefit from seeing all steps at once in a tab bar

categorizationOptions.description: All available options on the Categorization element when variant is 'stepper'

options.name: variant
options.description: Set to 'stepper' for a simple stepper with a navigation tab bar at the top

options.name: showNavButtons
options.description: When true, shows Next and Previous navigation buttons below the form content

options.name: nextButtonLabel
options.description: Custom text for the Next button

options.name: nextButtonType
options.description: GoA Design System button type for the Next button (e.g. 'primary', 'secondary', 'tertiary')

options.name: previousButtonLabel
options.description: Custom text for the Previous button

options.name: previousButtonType
options.description: GoA Design System button type for the Previous button

notes: The simple stepper shows all step labels in a navigation bar at the top — users can click any step
Set `showNavButtons: true` to add Next/Previous buttons for linear navigation
Customize button styling with `nextButtonType` and `previousButtonType` (GoA Design System button types)
The simple stepper does NOT have a Task List or section groupings — use `variant: "pages"` for that
A Summary page is automatically appended for applicant review
For most government citizen-facing forms, the page stepper (variant: pages) is preferred
CRITICAL: Category elements can ONLY be direct first-level children of the Categorization `elements` array. Do NOT wrap Categories in VerticalLayout, Group, or any other layout element
